content.value: To introduce yourself in Cantonese, you can use 我係 (ngo5 hai6) meaning 'I am' or 我叫 (ngo5 giu3) meaning 'I am called'. When asking someone else their name, the standard phrase is 你叫咩名？ (nei5 giu3 me1 meng2?).

content.translation: I am called / My name is

content.value: In casual settings, names are often prefixed with 阿 (aa3). For example, a person named Ming (明) would be called 阿明 (aa3 ming4).
After meeting someone for the first time, it is polite to say 好高興識到你 (hou2 gou1 hing3 sik1 dou2 nei5), which translates to 'Nice to meet you'.

content.romanization: hou2 gou1 hing3 sik1 dou2 nei5

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: nei5 hou2! ngo5 giu3 aa3 ming4.
lines.translation: Hello! My name is Ah Ming.

lines.speaker: B
lines.romanization: aa3 ming4, nei5 hou2. ngo5 hai6 aa3 ling4.
lines.translation: Ah Ming, hello. I am Ah Ling.

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: nei5 hou2! nei5 giu3 me1 meng2 aa3?

lines.speaker: B
lines.romanization: ngo5 giu3 aa3 koeng4. nei5 ne1?
lines.translation: My name is Ah Keong. And you?

lines.speaker: A
lines.cantonese: 我係阿芬。好高興識到你。
lines.romanization: ngo5 hai6 aa3 fan1. hou2 gou1 hing3 sik1 dou2 nei5.
lines.translation: I am Ah Fan. Nice to meet you.

pages.title: Lesson 2.2 Complete!
pages.summary: You can now introduce yourself, ask for someone's name, and express pleasure in meeting new people.
